notes: Spartan general known for his naval command during the Peloponnesian War, noted for ambition and harsh governance of Asian cities.

docs.title: Life of Agesilaus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: Indeed they were both blinded with the same passion, so as one not to recognize the authority of his superior, the other not to bear with the imperfections of his friend.

docs.title: Life of Lycurgus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … from the wars, although himself incorrupt, he yet by this means filled his country with avarice and luxury, and subverted the laws and ordinances of Lycurgus; so long as which were in force, the aspect presented by Sparta was rather that of a rule of life followed by one wise and

docs.title: Life of Lysander
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … he answered, “as his slave?” This ambitious temper was indeed only burdensome to the highest personages and to his equals, but through having so many people devoted to serve him, an extreme haughtiness and contemptuousness grew up, together with ambition, in his character.
